title: ulmus procera f. purpurea
intro: Ulmus procera f. purpurea, also known as the English elm, is a deciduous tree species that belongs to the family Ulmaceae. The medicinal properties of Ulmus procera f. purpurea lie in its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins and flavonoids, making it a natural astringent and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Ulmus procera f. purpurea is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used topically in the form of a cream or salve to harness its medicinal properties.
